title: ACC/AHA Guideline for the Diagnosis and Management of Patients With Non-ST-Elevation Acute Coronary Syndromes (NSTE-ACS)
source: American College of Cardiology / American Heart Association
description: Evidence-based guidelines for risk stratification, antiplatelet therapy, anticoagulation, invasive strategy, and secondary prevention in patients with NSTEMI and unstable angina. Includes specific guidance for allergy management in ACS.
recommendations: DUAL ANTIPLATELET THERAPY (DAPT): Aspirin 325mg loading dose PLUS P2Y12 inhibitor (ticagrelor 180mg or clopidogrel 600mg) for all NSTE-ACS patients
ASPIRIN ALLERGY PROTOCOL: If true aspirin allergy — do NOT administer aspirin; use P2Y12 inhibitor monotherapy (ticagrelor preferred) plus consider aspirin desensitization
ASPIRIN ALLERGY — ALTERNATIVE: Ticagrelor 180mg loading dose as monotherapy is acceptable when aspirin is contraindicated due to allergy
ASPIRIN HYPERSENSITIVITY: Aspirin desensitization protocol may be performed in catheterization lab setting under monitoring if aspirin essential for stenting
Obtain 12-lead ECG within 10 minutes of ED arrival; repeat every 15-30 min if initial non-diagnostic
Measure high-sensitivity troponin at presentation and 1-3 hours later (0/1hr or 0/3hr algorithm)
Risk stratification using TIMI or GRACE score within 2 hours of presentation
Anticoagulation: UFH or enoxaparin for all NSTE-ACS; fondaparinux preferred if no PCI planned
Early invasive strategy (angiography within 24h) for high-risk features: elevated troponin, dynamic ECG changes, GRACE score >140
Statin therapy: high-intensity statin (atorvastatin 40-80mg) immediately upon diagnosis
Beta-blocker within 24 hours if no contraindications (EF >=40%, no acute HF, no bradycardia)
ACE inhibitor/ARB for all NSTE-ACS with EF <40%, hypertension, or diabetes
Avoid NSAIDs and COX-2 inhibitors in ACS — increased cardiovascular risk
Secondary prevention: smoking cessation, cardiac rehabilitation, lipid management to LDL <70 mg/dL
